evidence: In October 2025, we entered into a product purchase agreement with OpenAI OpCo, LLC, (OpenAI) to deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs, with the deployment of the first gigawatt of capacity powered by our AMD Instinct MI450 series products.

We strengthened our AI leadership through a number of strategic acquisitions during the year. In March 2025, we acquired ZT Group Int l, Inc. (ZT Systems)... This acquisition enables us to deliver end-to-end AI solutions.
We continue to invest in software capabilities and the open ecosystem through the AMD ROCm platform, delivering new features for high-performance AI training and inference.
Demand for our data center AI accelerator products was strong as large hyperscale customers, OEMs and ODMs deployed our AMD Instinct MI350X Series GPUs.
In 2025, we launched AMD Ryzen AI 300 Series processors featuring a next generation NPU supporting Microsoft Copilot+ PCs... In January 2026, we launched our Ryzen AI 400 series processors.
In 2025, we introduced key optimizations and expanded support for frameworks and libraries, improving performance for generative AI workloads and simplifying the developer experience.

Nvidia Corporation (Nvidia) leverages its market position in data center GPU, financial resources, and proprietary software ecosystem to promote its systems and influences customers who do business with us.
Issues related to the responsible use of AI may result in reputational, competitive and financial harm and liability.
We may encounter competition from customers who internally develop products to support similar AI workloads to those supported by ours.

capex_mentions.quote: deploy 6 gigawatts of AMD GPUs, with the deployment of the first gigawatt of capacity powered by our AMD Instinct MI450 series products
capex_mentions.context: Multiyear strategic partnership with OpenAI for GPU deployment, announced October 2025

limitations: No explicit dollar-amount capex figures for AMD's own AI R&D or infrastructure build-out were found in the excerpts; the 6-gigawatt OpenAI agreement is a product purchase/deployment commitment rather than AMD's own capex, so intensity scored 4 rather than 5.
The OpenAI agreement is described as 'multiyear' which could support a score of 5, but no dollar value or multi-year capex program budget is quantified in the excerpts.

Narrative concreteness is scored 4 (deployed with operating detail) rather than 5 because no quantified business results (revenue figures, units shipped, % performance gains attributed to AI products) appear in the excerpts.

Risk disclosure scored 4 based on company-specific mechanisms (Nvidia ecosystem lock-in, customer self-development of AI chips, responsible-use liability) but no quantified risk exposure or named governance/oversight body was found in the excerpts.
